channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: North Carolina CEO uses personal helicopter to save 11-day-old baby from remote area ravaged by Helene
item.description: Zeb Hadley, a private helicopter owner and CEO of National Coatings, ended up saving an 11-day-old baby who was born prematurely just before Hurricane Helene struck.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: A look behind the scenes at the National Toy Hall of Fame
item.description: ROCHESTER, N.Y. (AP) — When curators at the National Toy Hall of Fame learned last fall that the Fisher-Price Corn Popper had been voted in as part of the class of 2023, they knew they had some serious work to do.
item.dc:creator: CAROLYN THOMPSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hurricane Milton ravaged one of the most popular areas for 'snowbirds' on Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: Hurricane Milton’s devastating landfall along Florida’s Gulf Coast hit one of the most popular areas for temporary residents who spend the winter months living in the Sunshine State.
item.dc:creator: Mike Schneider And Kate Payne, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: A man accused of threatening to kill Democratic election officials will likely plead guilty
item.description: DENVER (AP) — A man accused of repeatedly threatening to kill the top elections officials in Colorado and Arizona as well as judges and federal law enforcement agents is expected to plead guilty in federal court on Wednesday. Teak Ty Brockbank, 45, of Cortez, Colorado, has been jailed since his Aug.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN SLEVIN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Surfer dies after being struck, impaled by swordfish
item.description: A longtime surfer and travel guide died after being impaled by a swordfish off the coast of Indonesia.
item.dc:creator: Tony Kurzweil, KRON4

item.title: Yelp turns off comments on the McDonald's that Trump worked at after fake reviews
item.description: Yelp recently paused reviews on a McDonald's in Pennsylvania after former President Trump served pre-screened customers at the location Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WGN 9

item.title: Court upholds freedom for woman whose conviction was overturned after 43 years behind bars
item.description: An appellate court in Missouri has ruled that a lower court was right when it decided to overturn the murder conviction of a woman who spent 43 years behind bars for a killing that her attorneys argue was committed by a discredited police officer.
item.dc:creator: Heather Hollingsworth, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McDonald's Quarter Pounders linked to E. coli outbreak across multiple states, CDC says
item.description: The McDonald's Quarter Pounder has been linked to an E. coli outbreak across 10 states in which one person has died, according to the Centers for Disease Control and Prevention.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Accettulla, WFLA, NBC

item.title: LA City Council candidate says 'F--- the police' in leaked audio, prompting blowback from police union
item.description: A candidate for the Los Angeles City Council is under fire for saying, "F--- the police," when asked about her stance on funds for police overtime.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, ABC 4

item.title: Biden heaps praise on Slovenia PM for aiding release of Americans in major US-Russia prisoner swap
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden heaped praise on Slovenian Prime Minister Robert Golob on Tuesday for his role in the recent seven-nation prisoner swap that freed Americans Evan Gershkovich, Alsu Kurmasheva and Paul Whelan from Russian prison. The deal, completed in August, was the largest U.
item.dc:creator: AAMER MADHANI and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Grieving families of seniors killed when a dock collapsed say the tragedy was preventable
item.description: JACKSONVILLE, Fla. (AP) — Regina Brinson heard a crack before the metal walkway gave way beneath her feet, plunging her into the water beneath the state-operated ferry dock on Georgia's Sapelo Island.
item.dc:creator: RUSS BYNUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cartel using 'bracelets' to track whether migrants paid to cross
item.description: The Texas Department for Public Safety took NewsNation to a river where crews came across several discarded paper bracelets.
item.dc:creator: Ali Bradley, NewsNation

item.title: Judge orders Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: Rudy Giuliani must turn over much of his property — his apartment in New York, the city that made him mayor, and other prized possessions — to two Georgia election workers who have sought for months to collect their massive defamation judgment, a federal judge ruled Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, Fox 5

item.title: A Georgia teen accused in the Apalachee High School shooting has pleaded not guilty
item.description: ATLANTA (AP) — A 14-year-old Georgia boy indicted on murder charges in a mass shooting at Apalachee High School pleaded not guilty on Tuesday. Colt Gray's lawyer filed papers entering the plea after Gray was indicted on Thursday. They waived an arraignment hearing that had been scheduled for Nov.
item.dc:creator: JEFF AMY, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: Police said they haven't yet confirmed the cause and manner of death of the woman, who worked at the store, nor has her name been released.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, NewsNation

item.title: Asheville annual holiday tradition canceled due to Helene
item.description: ASHEVILLE, N.C. (WSPA) - An annual national holiday tradition that has taken place for over three decades in Asheville has been canceled due to the aftermath of Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Nikolette Miller, Fox 5

item.title: Judge orders Rudy Giuliani to hand over assets to Georgia election workers
item.description: The order appoints the election workers as receivers of Rudy Giuliani’s New York apartment, Mercedes-Benz, luxury watches and some of his cash, among other items.
item.dc:creator: Ella Lee, NewsNation

item.title: Ohio man charged with bringing massive 'Trump' sign to Capitol for rioters to use as a weapon
item.description: An Ohio man has been arrested on charges that he brought a massive “Trump” sign to the U.S. Capitol and joined other rioters in using it as a weapon against police officers during a mob attack.
item.dc:creator: Michael Kunzelman, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Insurance company refuses to pay 82-year-old Las Vegas woman's claim after her home burned down
item.description: Two weeks before the fire, the insurance company canceled the woman's policy, accusing her of running a motorcycle repair shop out of her home — something the 82-year-old woman denies.
item.dc:creator: Kyle J. Paine, Fox 5

item.title: Truck stolen in Syracuse with father's ashes still inside
item.description: SYRACUSE, N.Y. (WSYR-TV) -- A North Carolina man is pleading for help after his truck was stolen in Syracuse over the weekend. But what was inside the truck was the most troubling to him.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Cafaro, NewsNation

item.title: 19-year-old woman found dead in oven in Canada Walmart
item.description: A Canadian Walmart store was closed for a third day Tuesday as police investigated the death of a woman whose body was found inside an oven.
item.dc:creator: Wil Day, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Oscar forms in the Caribbean
item.description: The formation of Oscar and Nadine comes as parts of the U.S. have been hit hard by a pair of hurricanes, Helene and Milton, that devastated communities, destroyed roads, caused power outages, took lives and injured many.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: LIST: Waffles, pancakes recall expanded over potential listeria contamination
item.description: The recalled products were sold under multiple brand names across the entire country, the company says.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, Fox 5

item.title: Meta again testing facial recognition software to fight 'celeb bait' scams
item.description: Meta, the parent company of Facebook and Instagram, is testing facial recognition software to combat “celeb bait” scams on its platforms as well as to allow users to more easily regain access to their accounts.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4

item.title: Trump selling 'MAGADonald's' shirts after drive-thru appearance
item.description: After Trump’s visit to the Bucks County franchise, McDonald’s sought to emphasize its political neutrality and said it does not endorse political candidates.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Mike Jeffries, former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO, arrested on federal sex trafficking charges
item.description: Former Abercrombie & Fitch CEO Michael Jeffries and two other men have been arrested on sex trafficking and interstate prostitution charges, a spokesperson for federal prosecutors said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: New campus protest rules spur an outcry from college faculty
item.description: Dissent is thriving this fall at American colleges, and not just among student activists. With student protests limited by new restrictions, faculty have taken up the cause. To faculty, new protest rules threaten freedom of speech — and the freedom to think, both central to university life.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE MA, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Helicopter crashes into radio tower in Houston, killing 4
item.description: HOUSTON (AP) — Four people aboard a helicopter were killed when it crashed into a radio tower in Houston and burst into flames, officials said. The helicopter went down just before 8 p.m.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: PHOTOS: White House public tour gets makeover with 'reimagined' visitor experience
item.description: The White House public tour is getting an extreme makeover, with new digital exhibits, an expanded route and more access to rooms poised to be part of the "reimagined" experience for visitors.
item.dc:creator: Judy Kurtz, Fox 5

item.title: Zenni Optical offering free glasses for those affected by Hurricane Helene, Milton
item.description: Zenni Optical, a Nothern California company that sells prescription glasses online, is offering free glasses for those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Footage appears to show moment helicopter crash topples radio tower in Houston
item.description: A surveillance camera mounted outside of a Houston photo studio appears to have captured the moment a helicopter crash toppled a radio tower on Sunday, killing four people.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, Fox 5

item.title: Getting from Los Angeles to 2 major European cities just got easier
item.description: Adventurous SoCal residents looking to hop across the pond this summer can do so with ease thanks to one new and one reintroduced nonstop route from a relatively new low-cost airline.
item.dc:creator: Will Conybeare, KTLA 5

item.title: PHOTOS: Giant Pumpkin Regatta makes a splash
item.description: Paddlers dressed in their Halloween best raced in carved-out pumpkins with all their might as part of the West Coast Giant Pumpkin Regatta.
item.dc:creator: Jenna Deml, KRON4

item.title: Over 1 million voters cast early ballots in North Carolina
item.description: Nearly 1.01 million North Carolinians have already voted in the November general election, state officials reported on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Sophia Vento, Fox 5

item.title: How does abortion translate? Ballot measures challenge interpreters
item.description: Vague or technical terms can be challenging, even more so when it comes to Indigenous languages that have only limited written dictionaries.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 2 Navy aviators are declared dead after a fighter jet crashed in Washington state
item.description: Two crew members who were missing following the crash of a fighter jet in mountainous terrain in Washington state during a routine training flight have been declared dead, the U.S. Navy said Sunday.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, ABC 4
